task_completion.description: Open the input image hela-cells.tif for processing.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An image viewer or editor (e.g., Fiji/ImageJ) is open displaying the hela-cells.tif image. Note that despite the task description saying 'phase contrast', the image will likely appear as a composite fluorescence image (red, green, blue structures).

task_completion.description: Generate a binary mask where cells are white and background is black.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A processed version of the image is visible on screen consisting entirely of black (background) and white (cells) pixels, representing a segmentation of the cell areas.

task_completion.description: Calculate the confluence (percentage of area covered by cells).
task_completion.visual_evidence: A results table, measurement window, or output dialog showing an 'Area Fraction', '%Area', or similar confluence calculation. The value should be roughly around 49.5%.
A save dialog, file explorer window, or terminal command confirming the binary mask is being saved exactly as 'confluence_mask.png' in the '~/Fiji_Data/results/confluence/' directory.

task_completion.description: Record the measured confluence percentage in ~/Fiji_Data/results/confluence/confluence_report.txt.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal command writing the measured numeric value (e.g., ~49.51) into a file named 'confluence_report.txt' in the '~/Fiji_Data/results/confluence/' directory.

integrity.description: The agent used the GUI (e.g., Fiji/ImageJ) to process the image and extract the measurement.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the Fiji/ImageJ graphical interface (menus, thresholding tools, measure commands) rather than just running a pre-written script in the terminal.

integrity.description: The agent actually measured the confluence rather than guessing or hardcoding the ground truth value.
integrity.visual_evidence: The value written to the report text file matches a value seen in a measurement results window during the image processing steps.

integrity.description: The binary mask was genuinely generated from the input image.
integrity.visual_evidence: The shapes of the white areas in the binary mask visually correspond to the cell structures seen in the original hela-cells.tif image.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The input image 'hela-cells.tif' is actually a 512x672 composite fluorescence image (red lysosomes, green mitochondria, blue nucleus), despite the task description calling it 'phase contrast'. The ground truth confluence is approximately 49.51%. Acceptable measurements should fall within a 15% tolerance of this value (roughly 34.5% to 64.5%). The final mask must be strictly binary (white=255 for cells, black=0 for background).
